---
title: "Survival Analysis Mini-Course"
subtitle: "Time-to-event modelling for 3D medical imaging"
---

A build-along course that takes survival analysis from first principles to a
working 3D-imaging pipeline. Each section defines its terms before using them,
glosses notation on first appearance, and cross-links to the section that owns
each concept.

## The path

1. **[Background](01_background/1.1_what_is_survival_analysis.md)** — what
   survival analysis is, censoring, events, competing risks, and what clinicians
   actually want out of a model.
2. **[Loss functions](02_loss_functions/2.1_cox_ph_loss.md)** — Cox partial
   likelihood, the discrete-time negative log-likelihood, DeepHit, and how they
   compare.
3. **[Metrics](03_metrics/3.1_c_index.md)** — C-index, Brier score,
   Kaplan-Meier curves, time-dependent AUC.
4. **[Packages](04_packages/4.1_pycox.md)** — `pycox`, `lifelines`,
   `torchsurv`, `scikit-survival`, `auton-survival`, routed by how much of the
   training loop you own.
5. **[Recipes](06_recipes/6.1_pfs_stratification.md)** — end-to-end PFS risk
   stratification.

## How to read it

Start at the top and follow the cross-links. Notation is introduced once, where
the concept lives, and referenced everywhere else — so if a symbol is unfamiliar,
the link beside it points to its definition.

Figures throughout are generated deterministically from
[`scripts/make_figures.py`](scripts/make_figures.py); the synthetic examples are
chosen for clarity, and the runnable model code targets the built-in GBSG2
dataset.
